differences.title: 1. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Components render on the server, sending minimal JS for initial display.
differences.rightDesc: Components render in the browser, allowing user interaction quickly.

differences.title: 2. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Fetch data directly on the server without extra network hops.
differences.rightDesc: Fetch data from the browser using standard APIs like Fetch.

differences.title: 3. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Reduces JavaScript sent to the client, improving performance and load times.
differences.rightDesc: Includes JavaScript for rendering and interactivity on the client.

differences.title: 4. SEO
differences.leftDesc: Content is pre-rendered on the server, making it easily crawlable by search engines.
differences.rightDesc: SEO can be challenging if content is not rendered server-side.

differences.title: 5. INTERACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Primarily for rendering static or server-fetched content, not UI interactions.
differences.rightDesc: Handles all user interactions, state management, and dynamic UI updates.

differences.title: 6. ACCESS TO APIS
differences.leftDesc: Can access Node.js APIs, databases, and file system directly on the server.
differences.rightDesc: Limited to browser-based APIs and Web Storage.

differences.title: 7. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State is managed during server render, often stateless per request.
differences.rightDesc: Requires client-side state management solutions for dynamic applications.
